# Copilot Instructions — elibrary project

## Personal & relational

- The user is **Gerry Stahl**, a researcher and author. Address him as **Gerry** in conversation.
- Tone should be **collegial and concise** — treat this as a collaboration between equals, not a tutorial.
- Do not add unnecessary preamble, summaries, or "Great question!" filler.
- Do not produce unsolicited summary markdown files after completing a task.

---

## Project identity

This workspace is the **elibrary project**: a Python RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) system built around a **22-volume academic library** authored by Gerry Stahl, primarily on **computer-supported collaborative learning (CSCL)** and related topics (philosophy of mind, dynamic geometry, collaborative design).

The project has two several components:

1. **The RAG system** — FAISS + BM25 indexes, a Gradio web app (`scripts/main.py`) and a terminal REPL (`scripts/query_library.py`) for querying the library using local Ollama models (qwen2.5:14b, llama3.1:8b, gemma3:4b) and Anthropic Claude.

2. **The citation/cluster analysis pipeline** — an ongoing research analysis tracing the thematic development of Gerry's work through chunk-level clustering and self-citation timelines.

The Copilot assistant is acting as a **data analysis and programming collaborator** on this project — not a coding tutor.

---

## User identity

The project runs on a 2026 Macbook Pro with an Apple M5 Chip set, 32 GB Memory, 4 TB Storage

The User is trained in computer science, including AI, with a BS from MIT (1967) and a PhD from Colorado (1994).
He is also trained in philosophy, especially German social thought, at MIT, Heidelberg, Frankfurt, with a PhD from Northwestern ()
He is currently a wood sculptor and serves of the Town of Chatham's Energy and Climate Action Comittee. He lives on Cape Cod.
His website is: http://GerryStahl.net .
---

## Technical conventions

- **Python venv:** always use `.venv/bin/python` (never bare `python` or `python3`)
- **Working directory:** `/Users/GStahl2/AI/elibrary/`
- **Code style:** type hints on function signatures, module-level docstrings on all scripts, `pathlib.Path` for file paths, no raw `os.path`
- **API keys:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set in the environment; never hardcode keys
- **Claude model for labelling/summarisation:** `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`

### Key file paths

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `vector_store/elibrary.faiss` | 36,089 vectors, dim=768; level-0 = 17,441 base chunks |
| `vector_store/elibrary_meta.json` | `{vid: {book_number, chapter_number, chunk_level, chunk_page, chunk_words, book_title, chapter_title}}` |
| `vector_store/chunks_bm25/sidecar.json` | `{doc_id: {vector_id, …, chunk_text}}` — source of all chunk text |
| `cache/elibrary_cache.pkl` | `ElibraryCache` dataclass; `cache.books` is a **list**; each `Book` has `book_chapters` (not `chapters`); each `Chapter` has `chapter_reference` (APA string with pub year) |
| `reports/chunk_clusters.csv` | 17,441 rows: `vector_id, book_number, chapter_number, book_title, chapter_title, chunk_index, chunk_page, pub_year, cluster_id, cluster_label` |
| `reports/cluster_report.txt` | Full cluster report with paragraph summaries (generated by `expand_cluster_summaries.py`) |
| `reports/cluster_summaries.json` | `{cluster_id: {label, summary}}` — structured cluster metadata |
| `reports/self_citations.csv` | 2,852 rows: `vector_id, …, cluster_id, cluster_label, pub_year, cited_year, snippet` |
| `reports/self_citation_report.txt` | Citation timeline report by cluster |
| `reports/cluster_citation_scatter.png` | 20-panel scatter plot: citing year × cited year per cluster |
| `reports/global_citation_heatmap.png` | Full heatmap: all citing × cited years with marginals |
| `reports/citation_pairs.jsonl` | 2,852 citation pair records with resolution status (422 resolved, 14.8%) |
| `reports/citation_pairs_diagnostic.txt` | 600-line diagnostic report for the citation pair resolution pipeline |
| `reports/cluster_timelines.png` | Chapter-level publication timeline: plurality-cluster per chapter, 18 clusters, 1970–2026 |
| `reports/chunk_timelines.png` | Chunk-level publication timeline: each level-0 chunk in its own cluster, 15,629 chunks |
| `reports/timeline_data.csv` | Spreadsheet of raw counts + share % per cluster for both chapter and chunk timelines |
| `reports/narrative_chunks.json` | 16,727 cleaned narrative chunks (22.9 MB): `{vector_id, book/chapter, pub_year, cluster_id, narrative_text, …}` |
| `reports/style_features.csv` | 336 rows × 40 cols: one row per chapter, all stylometric features + pub_year + cluster_id |
| `memory/elibrary_memory.jsonl` | MCP knowledge graph (JSONL); 42 entities, 27 relations; read by `elibrary-memory` MCP server |
| `.vscode/mcp.json` | MCP server config — `elibrary-memory` via `npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory` |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | This file — auto-injected into every Copilot Chat session |
| `documents/project_status.md` | Auto-generated session status snapshot — regenerate with `generate_project_status.py` |

### Project vocabulary

- **level-0 chunk** — the base (finest-grained) chunk type in the FAISS index; `chunk_level == 0` in metadata
- **sidecar** — `chunks_bm25/sidecar.json`; the file that holds the actual chunk text
- **breadth** — mean cosine distance of a cluster's member vectors from its centroid; lower = tighter/more focused
- **relative breadth** — breadth / global mean breadth; >1.0 = broader than average
- **pub_year** — the publication year of the *citing* chapter (extracted from `chapter_reference`)
- **cited_year** — the year of the Stahl work being cited (extracted from inline citation text)

---

## Analysis pipeline — current state

### Completed
- **Step 1: Chunk clustering** — `scripts/cluster_chunks.py` — k=20 KMeans on level-0 vectors, labelled with Claude Haiku; outputs `chunk_clusters.csv` and `cluster_report.txt`
- **Cluster summaries expanded** — `scripts/expand_cluster_summaries.py` — rewrote `cluster_report.txt` with 3–5 sentence paragraph summaries; outputs `cluster_summaries.json`
- **Step 2: Self-citation extraction** — `scripts/extract_self_citations.py` — extracted 2,852 inline Stahl self-citation pairs from 1,085 chunks; outputs `self_citations.csv` and `self_citation_report.txt`
- **Step 3: Visualisation** — `scripts/visualize_self_citations.py` — per-cluster scatter grid and global heatmap
- **Step 2b: Self-citation pair resolution** (paused) — `scripts/build_citation_pairs.py` — attempted to resolve citations to specific cited chapters; 422/2,852 resolved (14.8%); paused because chapter-level vectors too coarse and ambiguous-year rate too high (38.7%)
- **Step 3b: Cluster publication timelines** — `visualize_cluster_timelines.py` (chapter) + `visualize_chunk_timelines.py` (chunk) + `export_timeline_data.py` (CSV); 18 clusters, 1970–2026; chunk-level preferred
- **Style analysis pipeline** — `build_narrative_chunks.py` → `narrative_chunks.json` (16,727 cleaned chunks); `analyze_style.py` → `style_features.csv` (336 chapters × 40 features, 1967–2026); 12 PNG visualisation reports via `visualize_style.py`, `visualize_style_extended.py`, `visualize_style_report56.py`
- **Long-term memory infrastructure** — `copilot-instructions.md` (auto-injected), `documents/project_status.md` (auto-generated snapshot), MCP knowledge graph (`memory/elibrary_memory.jsonl`, 42 entities, 27 relations, seeded by `scripts/seed_mcp_memory.py`)

### Pending
- **Step 4: Cross-cluster citation matrix** — which clusters' chunks cite works originating in which other clusters; requires matching `cited_year` back to the cluster of that year's chapters
- **Step 2b resumption** (if useful case identified): improving citation pair resolution rate by filtering monographs from `ambiguous_year` candidates
- **Style analysis Report 5 — vocabulary depth**: hapax ratio per decade, top-N content words as wordclouds (not yet started)

### Style analysis key findings (treat as established)
- **Sentences shorter over time**: early (1967-84) mean 22.3w → late (2016-26) 19.4w
- **`we_per_1k` collapsed**: 9.3 → 3.5 — early philosophical rhetorical "we" → CSCL collaborative register
- **`assert_per_1k` halved**: 4.9 → 2.2 — fewer "clearly/must/obviously" in mature academic prose
- **`causal_per_1k` dropped**: 1.6 → 0.4 — less explicit syllogistic scaffolding ("therefore/thus/hence")
- **Citation density exploded**: 0.95 → 5.1/1k — philosophical essay register → literature-heavy CSCL
- **`metalinguistic_per_1k` near zero** (global mean 0.05) — moves rarely announced explicitly ("this chapter argues")
- **Readability stable**: Flesch ≈ 30, FK grade ≈ 14, Fog ≈ 18 throughout — solidly graduate-level prose

### Key findings (treat as established)
- **2006** is by far the most-cited year (773 hits) — corresponds to *Group Cognition* (MIT Press)
- **Clusters 6 and 12** are bibliography/reference-list noise — their exemplars show raw citation lists, not prose; they should be excluded from content analysis
- **Cluster 10** (Tacit Knowledge in Design Systems) and **Cluster 14** (HERMES Language) are early-career work; cited year ranges top out at 1993–1995
- **Cluster 4** (VMT) has the most self-citations (638); 2021 alone accounts for 219 — consistent with a retrospective book
- **Cluster 5** (Perspectives/Knowledge Construction Systems): massive 2010 → 1999 citing pattern (124 hits) — a retrospective survey chapter
- **Clusters 9, 8, 15** are non-CSCL content (salt marsh conservation, electronic music, sculpture) — part of the library but peripheral to analysis

### Citation regex patterns (verified working)
Three patterns extract inline Stahl citations; reference list entries (`Stahl, G. (YYYY). Title…`) are correctly excluded because the initial `G.` breaks all three patterns:
```python
_PAT_STAHL_FIRST = re.compile(r'\bStahl(?:\s+et\s+al\.?|\s+&\s+[^,()]{1,40})?,\s*(\d{4}[a-z]?)\b')
_PAT_NARRATIVE   = re.compile(r'\bStahl\s+\((\d{4}[a-z]?)\b')
_PAT_PAREN_BROAD = re.compile(r'\(([^()]*\bStahl\b[^()]*)\)')
```

---

## Behavioural guardrails

- **Check existing scripts before writing a new one** — there are ~90 scripts; the task may already be done
- **Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones** for incremental changes
- **Never hardcode absolute paths** in scripts — always use `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` to find the workspace root
- **Do not rewrite working code** unless explicitly asked; prefer targeted edits
- **Terminal commands** use the full venv path: `.venv/bin/python script.py`
- When in doubt about the structure of the cache or FAISS data, read the relevant script (`build_cache.py`, `build_vector_store.py`) rather than assuming
- **When asked to "clean up", "wrap up", or "update the system"** — update `documents/GUIDE_TO_GERRY_RAG_QUERY_SYSTEM.md` to reflect any new scripts, completed steps, new findings, or changed file paths from the current session. Also move any completed pipeline steps from Pending → Completed in the Project Status section. Do the same for the Analysis Pipeline section of this file. Then run `.venv/bin/python scripts/generate_project_status.py` to regenerate `documents/project_status.md`.
- **At the start of an analysis session**, Gerry can attach `documents/project_status.md` with `#file` for a compact current-state snapshot, or attach the full Guide for deeper context. The status file is auto-generated (no manual editing needed except the Open Work Items section).
